---
title: "Application Review"
---

## Review scope

The review examined:

- session lifecycle and concurrency;
- microphone and archive boundaries;
- C bridge ownership;
- Voxtral streaming recovery;
- Gemma process isolation and validation;
- overlay geometry and scrolling;
- spoken command handling;
- HTTP parsing, authentication, rate limiting, and browser rendering;
- tests, packaging, and documentation.

## Corrected defect

### Paused audio still entered Voxtral and the WAV archive

The session phase changed to `paused`, so the domain model ignored provisional
and finalized events. However, the microphone callback wrote PCM to the archive
and sent it to Voxtral before checking session state.

Consequences included:

- recording speech the user believed was paused;
- accumulating model audio that could join speech after resume;
- finalizing buffered paused speech during stop;
- incrementing audio diagnostics while paused.

The fix adds a mutex-protected audio-input gate at the first line of the capture
processing handler. Pause closes it and requests a model boundary; resume opens
it. Stop, clear, and microphone failure close it. The gate prevents recording,
metrics, voice activity, and inference from seeing paused chunks.

## Review conclusions

No additional high-severity correctness or command-execution flaw was found in
the inspected paths. Important strengths are:

- immutable raw captions;
- bounded serialized correction;
- loopback-only Gemma endpoint;
- explicit model-output validator;
- no model tools;
- separate viewer and student capabilities;
- source-bound question tickets and layered rate limits;
- text-node browser rendering;
- serialized C stream ownership;
- structured bridge errors;
- overlay frames clamped to a visible display.

## Residual risks and test gaps

### Normal application termination during an active session

The custom menu calls service shutdown, but immediate process termination and
ordinary macOS Quit do not currently wait for model drain and archive
finalization. Users should stop the session before quitting. A future release
should implement an `NSApplicationDelegate` termination handshake that returns
`.terminateLater`, awaits `stopSession`, then replies to AppKit.

### Minimal HTTP parser

The parser is deliberately narrow and closes ordinary connections. It is
adequate for generated browser requests, but it is not a reusable RFC-complete
HTTP server. Future routes should either retain the same restricted grammar or
move to a maintained HTTP implementation. Fuzzing malformed headers,
`Content-Length`, and connection termination would strengthen confidence.

### Network address selection

The advertised URL prefers `en0` and otherwise the first active non-loopback
IPv4 address. Complex VPN, Ethernet-plus-Wi-Fi, or unusual interface ordering
can select an address that does not correspond to the constrained Wi-Fi
listener. Test institutional network configurations; a future implementation
can derive the address from the listener path/interface itself.

### One caption viewer

The SSE design intentionally accepts one live caption connection. This is not a
bug, but users may assume the QR supports a room of viewers. Supporting multiple
viewers requires per-connection backpressure, heartbeat, lifecycle, and
resource limits.

### Long-running C/Metal behavior

Automated tests do not load the full model on every run. The C engine still
needs:

- multi-hour soak testing;
- repeated load/free testing;
- sanitizer runs where compatible with Metal;
- allocation-failure hardening in upstream helper code;
- measurements after compiler, macOS, or model changes.

### Correction semantics

Structural validation cannot prove exact semantic equivalence. Raw fallback
limits the damage, but representative subject-specific corpora should remain
part of release testing.

## Verification performed

At the time this book was generated:

- the Swift package built;
- all 65 automated tests passed;
- plist syntax passed;
- diff whitespace checks passed;
- Quarto sources rendered to HTML and PDF;
- the packaged application was rebuilt.
